prerequisites: An active Mailjet API key pair, available on the API Key Management page (reachable via Account > API keys in the Mailjet dashboard). Mailjet generates the API Key and API Secret Key automatically.
The public API Key value, to supply as the connector's api_key field.
The matching API Secret Key value, to supply as the connector's api_key_secret field.

authMethods.name: API key and secret (HTTP Basic Auth)
authMethods.summary: Mailjet's Email API authenticates every request with HTTPS Basic Auth. The username is your API Key (the connector's api_key field) and the password is your API Secret Key (the connector's api_key_secret field). Both values come from the API Key Management page in your Mailjet account.

setupSteps.title: Open the API Key Management page
setupSteps.body: Sign in to your Mailjet account and go to Account > API keys to reach the API Key Management page. Mailjet generates an API Key and a matching API Secret Key automatically for your account.

setupSteps.title: Copy the API Key
setupSteps.body: Copy the API Key (the public key) from the API Key Management page. Enter it in the connector's api_key field. This value is used as the username for Mailjet's HTTPS Basic Auth.

setupSteps.title: Copy the API Secret Key
setupSteps.body: Copy the matching API Secret Key from the same page and enter it in the connector's api_key_secret field. This value is used as the password for Mailjet's HTTPS Basic Auth and must stay confidential.

sources.title: Mailjet Email API Overview (authentication and API Key Management)
